title: Report Salana, Anglo-Sanskrit School, Sham Churasi
gloss: Annual Report of the Anglo-Sanskrit School, Sham Churasi, for 1909-10.
publisher: Lala Naubat Rai, Head Master of the School
notes: Annual report of an Anglo-Sanskrit School — the DAV-style 'Anglo-Sanskrit' hybrid-curriculum institution (English + Sanskrit), the Arya-Samaj educational model; institutional ephemera.

title: Report wa Qwaid Sanatan Dharm Mandal, Punjab
gloss: The Annual Report of the Sanatan Dharm Mandal, Punjab, and its rules.
notes: Annual report and rules of the Sanatan Dharm Mandal — the orthodox-Hindu (Sanatanist) counter-organization to the reformist Arya Samaj; the intra-Hindu organizational field (Sanatan vs Arya) documented alongside the Arya-Samaj items. Reg 300 = copies 300 coincidence.

title: Sulh-i-Kul Peshwa, Tract No. 1
gloss: An exposition of the state of India at the time of Guru Nanak's birth.
notes: 'Universal Peace' (sulh-i-kul, the Akbar-era ecumenical ideal) tract on India at Guru Nanak's birth, 5000 copies — a Nanak-centred inter-religious-harmony message; the syncretic/pluralist counter-current to communal hardening (directly on-theme for the homogenization-vs-plurality dynamic). Very large run.

author: Sundar Singh
title: Guldasta-i-Harmonium, Hissa-i-Awwal
gloss: Instructions for playing the harmonium, Part I.
notes: A harmonium tutor, Part I, 2nd edition — DIRECTLY on the music-colonization thread: the harmonium (the colonial-introduced reed organ that reshaped North-Indian music practice) getting its own vernacular instruction manual; the instrument's vernacular pedagogy is exactly the mechanism of music's transformation. Flag for the music vertical.

title: Tarik
gloss: An Urdu translation of Smiles' 'Self Help' by Hasan Ali.
notes: Samuel Smiles' 'Self-Help' translated into Urdu — the foundational Victorian self-improvement gospel entering the vernacular (the ideology animating so many of this quarter's how-to/vocational manuals, e.g. Haft Ustad, Mashir-i-Ma'ash); a key index of the internalized improvement-ethic. The 'Self-Made Men' book at p24 s5 is a cognate.

author: Abdullah, Abad
title: Guldasta-i-Nasim-i-Jannat
gloss: Verses in praise of the prophet Muhammad.
notes: Na't (praise-of-the-Prophet) verse — opens the large URDU—POETRY section; the devotional-poetry staple.

author: Abdullah Khan
title: Haq Nama
notes: Hamd (praise-of-God) verse, tiny run of 92 copies. Note the English-named 'Mac Key Press' in Gujranwala.

author: Abnashi Ram
title: Astotar Sankat Niwaran
notes: Hindu devotional stotra ('Sankat Niwaran' = removal of distress, i.e. a Hanuman-type protective hymn) in Urdu script by a Hindu author — Hindu devotion in the Urdu-print medium.

author: Afzal Hussain, M.
title: Chamanistan-i-N'at
gloss: Verses in praise of the prophet Muhammad.
publisher: M. Muhammad Yahya, Muhammad Ashraf, Book-sellers
